name: NXP Semiconductors N.V.
business: NXP Semiconductors N.V. is a global semiconductor company providing solutions in embedded processing, mixed-signal analog-digital, power management, and security, used in automotive, industrial & IoT, mobile, and communication infrastructure end markets. It sells to leading global companies through direct sales and distributors, with revenue of $12,269 million in 2025.

exposures.sentence: Kinara acquisition complements and expands NXP s solutions for AI-powered edge systems in the Industrial & IoT and Automotive end markets.
TTTech Auto is a leader in innovating unique safety-critical systems and middleware for software-defined vehicles (SDVs).

We have a very broad portfolio of Analog and Interface products that are used in many markets, particularly Automotive, Industrial & IoT and Mobile. In Automotive, we are the market leader in most of the applications, with integrated 77Ghz Radar solution for ADAS.
Semiconductor content is also increasing in engine management and fuel economy applications, like Battery Management Systems (BMS).
In Communication Infrastructure & Other, we provide 5G radio power and digital network communication solutions.
NXP is the market leader in security controller ICs.
Kinara is an industry leader in high performance, energy-efficient and programmable discrete neural processing units (NPUs).
Industrial & IoT market is highly fragmented with a diverse collection of products and applications such as factory automation.

Enabling innovation at our customers as well as reducing complexity, integration efforts and shorten time to market is a key element of our strategy. We believe we have the broadest Arm processor portfolio in the industry, from microcontrollers to crossover processors and from application processors to communication processors.
Within enterprise and data-center communications infrastructure, our processors are used in switches, routers, SD-WAN access devices, Wi-Fi access points, and network security systems.
UWB, thanks to its unique precision, robustness, and reliability, is emerging as a secure, fine-ranging technology capable of enabling a wide range of innovative location-based user experiences.

Carbon emissions reduction efforts (global net zero emission commitments) will also be a key growth driver with large transformations expected of our energy systems. Factories, buildings and homes will need to rely much more on renewable energy (e.g., solar, wind) and increase efficient use of energy.
NXP Semiconductors N.V. is a global semiconductor company and a long-standing supplier in the industry, with over 70 years of innovation and operating history.

catalysts.sentence: On June 17, 2025, NXP announced the closing of the acquisition of 100% of TTTech Auto for $766 million in cash ($675 million net of cash acquired).

On October 24, 2025, NXP closed the previously announced acquisition of 100% of Aviva Links for $222 million in cash ($202 million net of cash acquired) and $26 million through the settlement of previously held investments in Aviva Links.
On October 27, 2025, NXP closed the previously announced acquisition of 100% of Kinara, Inc. for $284 million in cash ($283 million net of cash acquired).

catalysts.event: Sale of MEMS sensors business line completed
catalysts.sentence: On February 2, 2026, NXP completed the previously announced sale of our MEMS sensors business line for $900 million in cash before closing adjustments and up to an additional $50 million contingent upon the achievement of specified technical milestones.

catalysts.event: Initial production target for ESMC (Dresden fab)
catalysts.sentence: Initial production at ESMC is currently targeted to begin in 2028.

catalysts.event: Initial production target for VSMC (Singapore fab)
catalysts.sentence: Initial production at VSMC is currently targeted to begin in 2027.

tamClaims.claim: The semiconductor market totaled $791.7 billion in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: The semiconductor market totaled $791.7 billion in 2025.
